description: Alangium platanifolium, commonly called alangium or lobed-leaf alangium, is a large deciduous shrub or small tree of the dogwood family (some experts assert it belongs in its own family of Alangiaceae). It typically matures over time to 12-15’ tall and as wide. It grows in a vase-shape when young, but rounds with age. It is native to Japan and Korea. It is noted for its attractive foliage and its small but showy flowers (somewhat resembling white martagon lilies). Broad, yellowish-green, cordate
origin: CHN SX, CHN SA, JAP, CHN, CHC YN

common_problems: When stressed, the shrub is susceptible to many disease problems.
The most serious is dogwood anthracnose.
Other diseases include powdery mildew, leaf spot, canker, root rot, and leaf and twig blight.
This shrub is more vulnerable to diseases in hot summer climates, especially south of USDA  Zone 7.
The plant is weedy, and its suckering can make it difficult to remove.

common_pests: Leaf miner and scale are less serious potential insect pests.

description: The drupe emerges green, turns white, and then finally bluish-black by mid-summer. The stems of the fruits appear orangish-red. Each drupe contains a large seed. The fruits taste sour and bitter.

care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Ornamental, baskets, wood carving. Some species have edible fruits.
care.propagation_detail: Planting a row of hardwood cornus cuttings Cornus sanguinea , Cornus stolonifera , Cornus alba and Cornus sibirica strike well from hardwood cuttings taken in winter. Wait until all the leaves have fallen and then with a sharp and clean pair of secateurs remove a stem of pencil thickness. Cut the stem into lengths 15cm long. Make sure you know which is the bottom of each cutting. An easy way to avoid mistakes is to cut just above a bud at the top of the cutting at an angle. Insert the cuttings i

Spot anthracnose is more common.

The foliage is browsed by white-tailed deer, and these trees do not withstand polluti

care.used_for: ["This plant was used for medicinal purposes by Native Americans", "as well as for carvings", "looms", "and disinfectant. Wood is hard", "heavy", "close grained and strong. Often used for textile shuttles and spools as well as handles for tools."]
care.used_for_details: This plant was used for medicinal purposes by Native Americans, as well as for carvings, looms, and disinfectant. Wood is hard, heavy, close grained and strong. Often used for textile shuttles and spools as well as handles for tools.

care.used_for: ["The fruit is used in Chinese medicine. In China", "the fruit is known as  Zhu yu  or  Zao pi.  The fruit is made into a tonic", "and it may be prescribed for the treatment of impotence", "lumbago", "vertigo", "and night sweats."]
care.used_for_details: The fruit is used in Chinese medicine. In China, the fruit is known as  Zhu yu  or  Zao pi.  The fruit is made into a tonic, and it may be prescribed for the treatment of impotence, lumbago, vertigo, and night sweats.
care.edible_parts: Fruits are edible but astringent so may be best left for the birds.

care.light_guide: Climate: temperate
care.tips: Native to: CHS GD, CHS JX, CHN SX, CHN SA, CHS FJ | Similar plants: Lindera erythrocarpa
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Ornamental, baskets, wood carving. Some species have edible fruits.
care.propagation_detail: Planting a row of hardwood cornus cuttings Cornus sanguinea , Cornus stolonifera , Cornus alba and Cornus sibirica strike well from hardwood cuttings taken in winter. Wait until all the leaves have fallen and then with a sharp and clean pair of secateurs remove a stem of pencil thickness. Cut the stem into lengths 15cm long. Make sure you know which is the bottom of each cutting. An easy way to avoid mistakes is to cut just above a bud at the top of the cutting at an angle. Insert the cuttings i
